text:
Once upon a time in a quiet forest clearing, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He had a reputation for knowing all sorts of things, having witnessed many a sunrise and sunset. One particular evening, Oliver's keen eyes spotted a strange cloud streaking across the sky. Intrigued, he hooted softly, "Why do clouds dance like that?"

He hurredly gathered his friends: Clover, the drowsy squirrel; Poppy, the sleepy fox; and quiet Silvia, the wise old owl perched on a branch.

After several hours watching the clouds, they devised a plan. Oliver would fly overhead, counting them, while his friends kept watch for any changes.

First came a fluffy cotton ball. Oliver counted it, surprised. Next came a curious shape like several cotton balls intertwining. Once fifty fluffy orbs danced away into the twilight. Finally, a dense curtain appeared that seemed to dance on its own.
